sources.title: Reports of Brig. Gen. Alfred Iverson, C.S. Army, commanding brigade (No. 513)
sources.author: Alfred Iverson
sources.editionNote: OR vol. 27 part 2, pp. 578-582; both reports. Fetched dossier pass 10.
sources.note: The commander's own record of the destruction: the 100-yard closure, the 500 on the dress-parade line, the surrender-and-exoneration record, Halsey's rally. No clock in the report.

sources.title: Report of Maj. Gen. R. E. Rodes, C.S. Army, commanding division
sources.author: Robert E. Rodes
sources.editionNote: OR vol. 27 part 2, pp. 552-563. Fetched dossier pass 10.
sources.note: The division frame: launch order, O'Neal's instant repulse, 'His dead lay in a distinctly marked line of battle.'

sources.title: Report of Brig. Gen. S. D. Ramseur, C.S. Army, commanding brigade
sources.author: Stephen D. Ramseur
sources.editionNote: OR vol. 27 part 2, pp. 587-588. Fetched dossier pass 10.
sources.note: The rescuer's record: 'almost annihilated'; the en-masse flank attack that retrieved the field; 800-900 prisoners.

sources.title: Report of Brig. Gen. Henry Baxter, U.S. Army, commanding 2d Brigade, 2d Division, I Corps (No. 52)
sources.author: Henry Baxter
sources.editionNote: OR vol. 27 part 1, pp. 307-309. Fetched dossier pass 11.

clockProfile.assessment: report-nominal ~0 profile (dossier); seven stated clocks, none between 11:00 and 17:00

sources.note: The receiving line's record: the crest/wall line, 'a most deadly fire', the three-regiment charge, the flags, the 12th Massachusetts flank fire on the surrender.

sources.title: Report of Brig. Gen. John C. Robinson, U.S. Army, commanding 2d Division, I Corps (No. 43, with addenda)
sources.author: John C. Robinson
sources.editionNote: OR vol. 27 part 1, pp. 289-292 + addenda. Fetched dossier pass 11.

clockProfile.assessment: division grain; the grievance addenda carried beside the main report

sources.note: Division frame: <2,500 engaged, more than half lost; 'about 1,000 prisoners'; the rear-guard tail.

sources.title: Report of Col. Richard Coulter, Eleventh Pennsylvania Infantry (No. 44)
sources.author: Richard Coulter
sources.editionNote: OR vol. 27 part 1, pp. 292-295. Fetched dossier pass 11.

clockProfile.assessment: the 12:30 general-firing pin; the ~15:00 relief clock

sources.note: The interior Union clock: 'about 12.30 p.m. the firing became general'; relieved 'after 3 p.m.' with ammunition exhausted.

sources.title: Report of Brig. Gen. Lysander Cutler, U.S. Army, commanding 2d Brigade, 1st Division, I Corps
sources.author: Lysander Cutler
sources.editionNote: OR vol. 27 part 1, pp. 281-285. Fetched dossier pass 10.
sources.note: The Union left flank of the destruction: 'came in on their flank, and opened so hot a fire on them that one regiment threw down their arms and surrendered.'

sources.title: Return of casualties in the Army of Northern Virginia (OR 27/2 pp. 338-346), Iverson's brigade rows
sources.author: Army of Northern Virginia, medical director's office
sources.editionNote: p. 342: printed total row 130 k / 328 w / 308 m / 820 aggregate FAILS its own arithmetic (766); regiment rows 5th NC 31+112, 12th NC 10+46, 20th NC 29+93, 23rd NC 41+93; missing POOLED at brigade line (ED-49 pattern). ED-71 disposition: printed cells carried verbatim, arithmetic reconstruction (382 w) adopted for computation only.
sources.note: The EC6 exemplar's numbers: effectively all loss in the one Forney-field episode; the 308 missing IS the dress-parade line's capture mass.

sources.title: Rodes's division field return, June 30, 1863 (Carlisle)
sources.author: Rodes's division AAG
sources.editionNote: OR vol. 27 part 2, p. 564: Iverson 114 officers + 1,350 men PFD = 1,464. Primary over the tablet's 1,470 'present about' (6 apart, different measures).
sources.note: The engaged-strength primary for the brigade (dossier EC2, closed pass 11).

sources.title: War Department tablet: Iverson's Brigade, Rodes's Division (Oak Hill)
sources.author: Gettysburg National Military Park Commission
sources.editionNote: mon-iverson-hq at battlefield-local (3883.7, 8742.4), OSM-anchored (monument register, ED-27 verification); formation semantics with the Rodes division tablet beside it. Tablet arithmetic: '820 of 1,470'.

sources.title: War Department tablet: attack of Rodes's division, 2 P.M. July 1 (First Corps front)
sources.author: Gettysburg National Military Park Commission
sources.editionNote: 'made a vigorous attack at 2 P.M. with superior numbers along the entire line' (ED-26/ED-27 verification; CA-J1P-1's 14:00 adopted, envelope 13:30-14:45).
sources.note: The attack clock the destruction window hangs from (with Coulter's 12:30 and Cutler's 2 o'clock as the interior ladder).

sources.title: Bachelder first-day sheet, 2 P.M. position (j1-09)
sources.author: John B. Bachelder
sources.editionNote: Georeferenced via reconstruction/spatial (bachelder-manifest j1-09); reads taken 2026-07-12 (dossier pass 10): red 'IVERSON, N.C.' bar with regimental numbers at (3728, 8365); 'J. FORNEY' farm at (3614, 8637); blue 'BAXTER'S BRIGADE' bar at the Mummasburg-road wall (4007, 8424).
sources.note: The drawn mid-advance state and the drawn receiving wall — the two position reads the viewpoint geometry hangs on.
